back_translation: And it happened, when he finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was bound to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as himself.
And Saul took him that day and did not let him return to his father's house.
And Jonathan and David made a covenant, because he loved him as himself.
And Jonathan stripped off the robe that was on him and gave it to David, along with his garments, and even his sword and his bow and his belt.
And David went out in all that Saul sent him to do, and he prospered. And Saul set him over the men of war, and it was good in the eyes of all the people and also in the eyes of Saul's servants.

And it happened, when they came home, when David returned from striking the Philistine, that the women went out from all the cities of Israel to sing and to dance to meet Saul the king, with tambourines, with joy, and with three-stringed instruments.
And the dancing women answered and said, 'Saul has struck his thousands, and David his ten thousands.'
And it burned to Saul greatly, and this word was evil in his eyes, and he said, 'They have given to David ten thousands, but to me they have given the thousands. What more for him but the kingdom?'
And Saul was watching David with suspicion from that day onward.

And it happened on the next day, an evil spirit from God rushed upon Saul, and he prophesied in the midst of the house, and David was playing with his hand as day by day, and the spear was in Saul's hand.
And Saul hurled the spear and said, 'I will strike David and the wall.' But David turned aside from his face twice.
And Saul was afraid before David, because the LORD was with him, and from with Saul He had departed.
And Saul removed him from with himself, and set him for himself as commander of a thousand. And he went out and came in before the people.
And David was in all his ways prospering, and the LORD was with him.
And Saul saw that he was prospering greatly, and he was afraid before him.
And all Israel and Judah loved David, because he was going out and coming in before them.

And Saul said to David, 'Behold, my older daughter Merab — her I will give to you for a wife. Only be for me a son of valor, and fight the wars of the LORD.' But Saul said, 'Let not my hand be upon him, but let the hand of the Philistines be upon him.'
And David said to Saul, 'Who am I, and who is my life, the family of my father in Israel, that I should be son-in-law to the king?'
And it happened at the time of giving Merab daughter of Saul to David — and she was given to Adriel the Meholathite for a wife.
And Michal daughter of Saul loved David. And they told Saul, and the matter was right in his eyes.

And Saul said, 'I will give her to him, and she will be to him for a snare, and the hand of the Philistines will be on him.' And Saul said to David, 'For a second time you can be my son-in-law today.'
And Saul commanded his servants, 'Speak to David privately, saying, Behold, the king is pleased with you, and all his servants love you. And now, become son-in-law to the king.'
And Saul's servants spoke in the ears of David these words. And David said, 'Is it a small thing in your eyes to be son-in-law to the king? And I am a poor and lightly esteemed man.'
And Saul's servants reported to him saying, 'According to these words David has spoken.'

And Saul said, 'Thus you shall say to David: The king does not desire a bride-price, but a hundred foreskins of the Philistines, to take vengeance on the enemies of the king.' But Saul was thinking to cause David to fall by the hand of the Philistines.
And his servants reported to David these words, and the matter was right in the eyes of David to be son-in-law to the king. And the days were not yet fulfilled.

And David arose and went — he and his men — and struck among the Philistines two hundred men, and David brought their foreskins and they gave them in full to the king to become son-in-law to the king. And Saul gave him Michal his daughter for a wife.
And Saul saw and knew that the LORD was with David, and Michal daughter of Saul loved him.
And Saul continued to fear before David yet more, and Saul was David's enemy all the days.
And the commanders of the Philistines went out, and it happened, as often as they went out, that David prospered more than all the servants of Saul, and his name was very highly esteemed.
